# SwiftXMLCoder

> High-performance, Codable-compatible XML encoder/decoder for Swift.
> **Not the same as [CoreOffice/XMLCoder](https://github.com/CoreOffice/XMLCoder)** — different library, different API.
> SPM-only. macOS, iOS, tvOS, watchOS, Linux. Backed by libxml2. No Foundation XML APIs.

## Installation

```swift
// Package.swift
dependencies: [
    .package(url: "https://github.com/MFranceschi6/swift-xml-coder.git", from: "2.0.0")
],
targets: [
    .target(
        name: "MyTarget",
        dependencies: [
            .product(name: "SwiftXMLCoder", package: "swift-xml-coder"),
            // For macro support (Swift 5.9+):
            .product(name: "SwiftXMLCoderMacros", package: "swift-xml-coder"),
        ]
    )
]
```

---

## Pattern 1 — Encode / Decode a struct

```swift
import SwiftXMLCoder

struct Book: Codable {
    var title: String
    var author: String
    var year: Int
}

// Encode
let book = Book(title: "Swift in Practice", author: "Apple", year: 2024)
let xmlData = try XMLEncoder().encode(book)
// <Book><title>Swift in Practice</title><author>Apple</author><year>2024</year></Book>

// Decode
let decoded = try XMLDecoder().decode(Book.self, from: xmlData)
```

The root element name is the type name by default. Override with `XMLRootNode`.

---

## Pattern 2 — Macro API: @XMLCodable (Swift 5.9+)

`@XMLCodable` synthesises field-mapping. Annotate individual properties to override defaults.

```swift
import SwiftXMLCoder
import SwiftXMLCoderMacros

@XMLCodable
struct Product: Codable {
    @XMLAttribute var id: String    // → <Product id="SKU-1">
    var name: String                // → <name>Widget</name>  (default: element)
    @XMLText var price: Double      // → text content of <price>9.99</price>
    @XMLIgnore var cache: [String]? // excluded from XML entirely (must be Optional)
    @XMLCDATA var description: String // → <description><![CDATA[...]]></description>
}
// Output: <Product id="SKU-1"><name>Widget</name><price>9.99</price><description><![CDATA[...]]></description></Product>
```

**Without macros** use property wrappers directly:
```swift
struct Product: Codable {
    @XMLAttribute var id: String
    var name: String
}
```

---

## Pattern 3 — Streaming decode with XMLItemDecoder (large feeds)

Use `XMLItemDecoder` instead of `XMLDecoder` for documents where items repeat at the top
level and memory is a concern. It decodes one item at a time in O(1) memory.

```swift
struct Product: Decodable { let sku: String; let price: Double }

// Synchronous — returns [Product]
let products = try XMLItemDecoder().decode(
    Product.self, itemElement: "Product", from: catalogData
)

// Async — one item at a time (macOS 12+ / Swift 5.5+)
for try await product in XMLItemDecoder().items(
    Product.self, itemElement: "Product", from: catalogData
) {
    await persist(product)
}
```

---

## Pattern 4 — Namespaces

```swift
import SwiftXMLCoderMacros

// Root namespace via macro:
@XMLRootNamespace("http://schemas.example.com/orders/v2")
@XMLCodable
struct Order: Codable {
    var id: String
    // Per-field namespace override:
    @XMLFieldNamespace("http://schemas.example.com/shipping", prefix: "ship")
    var shippingAddress: String
}
```

Without macros, conform to `XMLRootNode` and `XMLFieldNamespaceProvider` manually.

---

## Pattern 5 — Untrusted / external input (security)

Always use the security preset when parsing data from untrusted sources.
It caps document depth, total node count, and text-node size.

```swift
let configuration = XMLTreeParser.Configuration(
    limits: .untrustedInputDefault()
)
let tree = try XMLTreeParser(configuration: configuration).parse(data: untrustedInput)
let decoded = try XMLDecoder().decode(MyType.self, from: untrustedInput)
// XMLDecoder also accepts a custom XMLTreeParser.Configuration via its configuration property.
```

---

## Choosing the right API

| Need | API |
|------|-----|
| Encode/decode a Codable type | `XMLEncoder` / `XMLDecoder` |
| Decode a large feed item-by-item | `XMLItemDecoder` |
| Parse raw events (SAX-style) | `XMLStreamParser` |
| Write raw events | `XMLStreamWriter` |
| Parse into a tree for XPath / transforms | `XMLTreeParser` → `XMLTreeDocument` |
| Deterministic / canonical XML | `XMLDefaultCanonicalizer` |
| XPath queries | `XMLDocument.xpathFirstNode(_:)` |

---

## Common mistakes

- **Do not import `Foundation.XMLElement`** — it is unrelated. SwiftXMLCoder types are `XMLTreeElement`, `XMLStreamEvent`, etc.
- **`@XMLCodable` is required** for `@XMLAttribute`, `@XMLText`, `@XMLIgnore`, `@XMLCDATA`, `@XMLFieldNamespace` to synthesise field mappings. Without it the property wrappers still work but macros do nothing.
- **`@XMLIgnore` fields must be `Optional`** or have a default value — the decoder will not find a value for them in XML.
- **`XMLItemDecoder`, not `XMLDecoder`, for streaming** — `XMLDecoder` loads the full tree; only `XMLItemDecoder` is memory-bounded.
- **Key transform strategies** — if the XML uses `snake_case` or `kebab-case` element names, set `XMLDecoder.Configuration.keyTransformStrategy` (e.g. `.convertFromSnakeCase`).
- **Errors** — all errors are typed as `XMLParsingError`. `decodeFailed` includes the coding path and source location.

---
